perturbation.original_text: 3. Term and Termination: This term of this Agreement commences on the Effective Date and terminates on August 2nd, 2015 upon completion of event. ANTHEMIC may at any time terminate this Agreement if the Sponsor breaches any material term or provision of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Term and Termination: This term of this Agreement commences on the Effective Date and terminates on August 2nd, 2015 upon completion of event. ANTHEMIC may terminate this Agreement only if the Sponsor breaches any material term or provision of this Agreement and after providing a 30-day written notice and opportunity to cure. However, ANTHEMIC may also terminate this agreement for any reason with no cause, as long as they inform the Sponsor with a 30-day written notice.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding ANTHEMIC's termination rights. The original text states ANTHEMIC may terminate only if the Sponsor breaches the agreement. The added text, however, states ANTHEMIC may terminate this agreement for any reason with no cause, as long as they inform the Sponsor with a 30-day written notice. This creates uncertainty on the conditions under which ANTHEMIC can terminate the agreement, conflicting the original termination condition, and the new termination condition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Term and Termination: This term of this Agreement commences on the Effective Date and terminates on August 2nd, 2015 upon completion of event. ANTHEMIC may terminate this Agreement only if the Sponsor breaches any material term or provision of this Agreement and after providing a 30-day written notice and opportunity to cure. However, ANTHEMIC may also terminate this agreement for any reason with no cause, as long as they inform the Sponsor with a 30-day written notice.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clear contradiction regarding the termination rights of ANTHEMIC. The original text and the first part of the changed text state ANTHEMIC may terminate only if the Sponsor breaches the agreement. However, the added text states ANTHEMIC may also terminate this agreement for any reason with no cause, as long as they inform the Sponsor with a 30-day written notice. This creates a direct conflict and inconsistency regarding the conditions under which ANTHEMIC can terminate the agreement, leading to uncertainty and potential unenforceability as it's unclear which condition prevails. This aligns with the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 11. Insurance. Without limiting or qualifying the Sponsor's liabilities, obligations, or indemnities, before the Event, the Sponsor will obtain, at its sole cost and expense, a comprehensive general liability insurance policy from a company acceptable to ANTHEMIC and authorized to do business in the state of Illinois with limits of no less than $1,000,000.00 per occurrence and $2,000,000.00 as an annual aggregate. The insurance mentioned in the preceding sentence will name ANTHEMIC as additional insured. The Sponsor will also maintain any statutorily required workers compensation insurance. The Sponsor will provide ANTHEMIC with properly executed certificates of insurance before the Sponsor provides any products or services at the Event, and the insurance will contain a provision that it cannot be reduced or cancelled unless and until the insurance company notifies ANTHEMIC. Any third party that performs services on the Event grounds on behalf of the Sponsor will be required to satisfy the same insurance requirements as provided in this section.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. Insurance. Without limiting or qualifying the Sponsor's liabilities, obligations, or indemnities, before the Event, the Sponsor may obtain, at its sole cost and expense, a comprehensive general liability insurance policy from a company acceptable to ANTHEMIC and authorized to do business in the state of Illinois with limits of no less than $1,000,000.00 per occurrence and $2,000,000.00 as an annual aggregate. The insurance mentioned in the preceding sentence will name ANTHEMIC as additional insured. The Sponsor will also maintain any statutorily required workers compensation insurance. The Sponsor will provide ANTHEMIC with properly executed certificates of insurance before the Sponsor provides any products or services at the Event, and the insurance will contain a provision that it cannot be reduced or cancelled unless and until the insurance company notifies ANTHEMIC. Any third party that performs services on the Event grounds on behalf of the Sponsor will be required to satisfy the same insurance requirements as provided in this section. Notwithstanding the foregoing, ANTHEMIC holds the right to waive these insurance requirements.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "will obtain" to "may obtain" and adding "Notwithstanding the foregoing, ANTHEMIC holds the right to waive these insurance requirements.", the clause now contradicts itself. Initially, it seems insurance is required, setting specific parameters. However, the addendum provides ANTHEMIC the right to waive insurance requirements. This directly contradicts the earlier part of the clause that implies a mandatory insurance obligation, since ANTHEMIC now has complete discretion to allow or disallow the insurance coverage. The main section that establishes insurance requirements in section 11, directly conflicts the addendum in the same section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. Insurance. Without limiting or qualifying the Sponsor's liabilities, obligations, or indemnities, before the Event, the Sponsor may obtain, at its sole cost and expense, a comprehensive general liability insurance policy from a company acceptable to ANTHEMIC and authorized to do business in the state of Illinois with limits of no less than $1,000,000.00 per occurrence and $2,000,000.00 as an annual aggregate. The insurance mentioned in the preceding sentence will name ANTHEMIC as additional insured. The Sponsor will also maintain any statutorily required workers compensation insurance. The Sponsor will provide ANTHEMIC with properly executed certificates of insurance before the Sponsor provides any products or services at the Event, and the insurance will contain a provision that it cannot be reduced or cancelled unless and until the insurance company notifies ANTHEMIC. Any third party that performs services on the Event grounds on behalf of the Sponsor will be required to satisfy the same insurance requirements as provided in this section. Notwithstanding the foregoing, ANTHEMIC holds the right to waive these insurance requirements.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'will obtain' to 'may obtain' coupled with the addition of ANTHEMIC's right to waive insurance requirements directly contradicts the initial implication of a mandatory insurance obligation within the same section. The amended clause now presents conflicting instructions regarding insurance, as it initially suggests a requirement before granting ANTHEMIC the discretion to waive it. This creates a strong in-text contradiction, and uncertainty, thus making it unenforceable because a reader would not understand whether it is mandatory to obtain insurance or not.

perturbation.original_text: 14. LIMITATION OF DAMAGES. EXCEPT FOR INDEMNIFICATION OBLIGATIONS DUE TO LIABILITIES TO THIRD PARTIES, NOTWITHSTANDING ANY PROVISION CONTAINED IN THIS AGREEMENT TO THE CONTRARY, NO PARTY TO THIS AGREEMENT WILL BE LIABLE TO ANY OTHER PARTY TO THIS AGREEMENT FOR ANY INCIDENTAL, INDIRECT, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES (INCLUDING DAMAGES FOR LOSS OF USE, POWER, BUSINESS GOOD WILL, REVENUE OR PROFIT, NOR FOR INCREASED EXPENSES, OR BUSINESS INTERRUPTION) ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THE PERFORMANCE OR NON PERFORMANCE OF THIS AGREEMENT UNLESS THE DAMAGES AROSE DUE TO A PARTY'S GROSS NEGLIGENCE OR WILLFUL BREACH OF THIS AGREEMENT.
perturbation.changed_text: 14. LIMITATION OF DAMAGES. EXCEPT FOR INDEMNIFICATION OBLIGATIONS DUE TO LIABILITIES TO THIRD PARTIES, NOTWITHSTANDING ANY PROVISION CONTAINED IN THIS AGREEMENT TO THE CONTRARY, NO PARTY TO THIS AGREEMENT WILL BE LIABLE TO ANY OTHER PARTY TO THIS AGREEMENT FOR ANY INCIDENTAL, INDIRECT, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES (INCLUDING DAMAGES FOR LOSS OF USE, POWER, BUSINESS GOOD WILL, REVENUE OR PROFIT, NOR FOR INCREASED EXPENSES, OR BUSINESS INTERRUPTION) ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THE PERFORMANCE OR NON PERFORMANCE OF THIS AGREEMENT. This limitation does not apply to damages arising from ANTHEMIC's failure to provide the agreed upon sponsorship benefits as outlined in Section 1.
perturbation.explanation: The original text has an exception for gross negligence or willful breach. However, the added text removes the 'UNLESS THE DAMAGES AROSE DUE TO A PARTY'S GROSS NEGLIGENCE OR WILLFUL BREACH OF THIS AGREEMENT' clause at the end and replaces it with "This limitation does not apply to damages arising from ANTHEMIC's failure to provide the agreed upon sponsorship benefits as outlined in Section 1." This creates a contradiction, suggesting ANTHEMIC is liable for one set of conditions, whereas the prior statement limited it from most damages.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 14. LIMITATION OF DAMAGES. EXCEPT FOR INDEMNIFICATION OBLIGATIONS DUE TO LIABILITIES TO THIRD PARTIES, NOTWITHSTANDING ANY PROVISION CONTAINED IN THIS AGREEMENT TO THE CONTRARY, NO PARTY TO THIS AGREEMENT WILL BE LIABLE TO ANY OTHER PARTY TO THIS AGREEMENT FOR ANY INCIDENTAL, INDIRECT, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES (INCLUDING DAMAGES FOR LOSS OF USE, POWER, BUSINESS GOOD WILL, REVENUE OR PROFIT, NOR FOR INCREASED EXPENSES, OR BUSINESS INTERRUPTION) ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THE PERFORMANCE OR NON PERFORMANCE OF THIS AGREEMENT. This limitation does not apply to damages arising from ANTHEMIC's failure to provide the agreed upon sponsorship benefits as outlined in Section 1.
perturbation.justification: The original text limited liability for incidental, indirect, special, consequential, or punitive damages except in cases of gross negligence or willful breach. The changed text removes this exception and adds a new exception for damages arising from ANTHEMIC's failure to provide sponsorship benefits. Since the general limitation of damages in the changed text now excludes damages from ANTHEMIC's failure to provide sponsorship benefits as outlined in Section 1, while the original limitation included a general exception only for gross negligence or willful breach, there is a contradiction. The contradicted text section is identical to the changed text, therefore meaning they are contradicting each other. The new exception effectively creates an additional liability condition beyond gross negligence or willful breach, thus creating a clear conflict with the original intent of the liability limitation and triggering a structural flaw leading to an in-text contradiction.
